question.text: Mrs. Patel invests Rs. 1,00,000 in a bank FD at 7% per year for 1 year (simple interest). How much will she receive at the end of 1 year?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Simple interest for 1 year = Principal x Rate x Time = Rs. 1,00,000 x 7/100 x 1 = Rs. 7,000. Total amount at end of 1 year = Rs. 1,00,000 + Rs. 7,000 = Rs. 1,07,000. **The Trap:** Rs. 1,70,000 would imply a 70% return — far too high. Rs. 1,00,700 uses 0.7% instead of 7%. Always be careful with decimal placement in percentage calculations. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under return calculation basics. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Many retired clients understand FD returns well. Use FD returns as a comparison point: "Your FD gives Rs. 7,000 on Rs. 1 lakh. A good debt fund might give Rs. 8,000-9,000 — and could be more tax efficient." This makes mutual funds relatable.
